channel.title: CPAN Security Group — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by CPAN Security Group (cpansec).

item.title: CVE-2026-12740 — Plack::Middleware::OAuth versions through 0.10 for Perl do not support the OAuth 2.0 state parameter
item.description: Plack::Middleware::OAuth versions through 0.10 for Perl do not support the OAuth 2.0 state parameter. RequestTokenV2 builds the provider authorization redirect without issuing a state value, and AccessTokenV2 exchanges the callback code and registers…

item.title: CVE-2026-12746 — Dancer2::Plugin::Auth::OAuth::Provider versions before 0.23 for Perl do not support the OAuth 2.0 state parameter
item.description: Dancer2::Plugin::Auth::OAuth::Provider versions before 0.23 for Perl do not support the OAuth 2.0 state parameter. The authentication_url method builds the provider authorization redirect without issuing a state value, and the callback method exchang…

item.title: CVE-2026-56015 — Net::IP::LPM versions through 1.10 for Perl allow a heap out-of-bounds read via an unbounded prefix length
item.description: Net::IP::LPM versions through 1.10 for Perl allow a heap out-of-bounds read via an unbounded prefix length. add() passes the prefix string to the trie builder addPrefixToTrie() without checking it against the address width. addPrefixToTrie() then wal…

item.title: CVE-2025-15646 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — HTML::Gumbo versions before 0.19 for Perl disclose heap memory via type confusion
item.description: HTML::Gumbo versions before 0.19 for Perl disclose heap memory via type confusion. Support for the <template> element was added to libgumbo 0.10.0 in 2015, but the walk_tree function in lib/HTML/Gumbo.xs was not updated to support it. The element was…

item.title: CVE-2026-56016 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — CGI::Session::ID::md5 versions before 4.49 for Perl generate predictable session ids from low-entropy sources
item.description: CGI::Session::ID::md5 versions before 4.49 for Perl generate predictable session ids from low-entropy sources. The generate_id method builds the session id from a MD5 digest of the process id, the epoch time, and the built-in rand() function. All thr…

item.title: CVE-2026-13766 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DBIx::QuickORM versions before 0.000026 for Perl allow SQL injection via unquoted SQL identifiers
item.description: DBIx::QuickORM versions before 0.000026 for Perl allow SQL injection via unquoted SQL identifiers. The default SQL builder, a SQL::Abstract subclass, sets bindtype in its constructor but never quote_char, so SQL::Abstract emits identifiers verbatim.…

item.title: CVE-2026-57082 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl generate the MSE Diffie-Hellman private key with a non-cryptographic PRNG
item.description: Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl generate the MSE Diffie-Hellman private key with a non-cryptographic PRNG. The MSE (Message Stream Encryption) handshake derives its 160-bit Diffie-Hellman private key from Perl's rand(), a non-cryptogr…

item.title: CVE-2026-57081 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl allow remote memory exhaustion via deeply nested bencoded input
item.description: Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl allow remote memory exhaustion via deeply nested bencoded input. bdecode recurses once per nested list or dictionary level with no depth cap, and each recursive call receives the remaining buffer by val…

item.title: CVE-2026-57080 — Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl allow remote memory exhaustion via an uncapped peer-wire message-length prefix
item.description: Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl allow remote memory exhaustion via an uncapped peer-wire message-length prefix. The peer-wire framing in _process_messages trusts the 4-byte length prefix sent by a connected peer with no upper bound, w…

item.title: CVE-2026-57079 — Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl write files outside the download directory via path traversal in peer-supplied metadata
item.description: Net::BitTorrent versions through 2.0.1 for Perl write files outside the download directory via path traversal in peer-supplied metadata. Net::BitTorrent validates file path components only on the .torrent-file ingest path. The peer and magnet metadat…

item.title: CVE-2026-13758 — CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl compare AEAD authentication tags in non-constant time in the streaming decrypt_done path
item.description: CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl compare AEAD authentication tags in non-constant time in the streaming decrypt_done path. The decrypt_done($tag) form compares it against the computed tag with memNE (memcmp() != 0), which short-circuits on t…

item.title: CVE-2026-56018 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — JavaScript::Minifier::XS versions before 0.16 for Perl leak memory on every call to minify(), allowing unbounded memory growth
item.description: JavaScript::Minifier::XS versions before 0.16 for Perl leak memory on every call to minify(), allowing unbounded memory growth. In JsMinify (XS.xs) the cleanup frees only the NodeSet structures and never the per-token contents buffers allocated in Js…

item.title: CVE-2026-56017 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — JavaScript::Minifier::XS versions before 0.16 for Perl crash with a NULL pointer dereference when the first meaningful token of the input is a slash
item.description: JavaScript::Minifier::XS versions before 0.16 for Perl crash with a NULL pointer dereference when the first meaningful token of the input is a slash. The regexp versus division disambiguator in JsTokenizeString (XS.xs) inspects the previous token's l…

item.title: CVE-2026-13593 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — CSS::Minifier::XS versions before 0.14 for Perl have a memory leak when the entire document is minified away
item.description: CSS::Minifier::XS versions before 0.14 for Perl have a memory leak when the entire document is minified away. The minify function has a memory leak when processing a document containing only characters to be removed, such as comments and whitespace.

item.title: CVE-2026-11702 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Bytes::Random::Secure::Tiny versions through 1.011 for Perl share internal state across forked processes
item.description: Bytes::Random::Secure::Tiny versions through 1.011 for Perl share internal state across forked processes. When an object is initialised before forking, then the internal state for the PRNG is shared across processes and identical random streams will…

item.title: CVE-2026-11625 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Bytes::Random::Secure versions through 0.29 for Perl share internal state across forked processes
item.description: Bytes::Random::Secure versions through 0.29 for Perl share internal state across forked processes. When an object is initialised before forking, or when the functional interface is used, then the internal state for the PRNG is shared across processes…

item.title: CVE-2026-12844 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — List::SomeUtils::XS versions before 0.59 for Perl have a heap buffer overflow in the pairwise function
item.description: List::SomeUtils::XS versions before 0.59 for Perl have a heap buffer overflow in the pairwise function. pairwise() collects the values returned by the block into a heap buffer sized to the longer input array, then grows the buffer before each copy wi…

item.title: CVE-2026-9733 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Mojolicious::Plugin::Web::Auth::OAuth2 versions through 0.17 for Perl have an insecure default state parameter
item.description: Mojolicious::Plugin::Web::Auth::OAuth2 versions through 0.17 for Perl have an insecure default state parameter. When no state generator is specified in the constructor, the module defaults to using a SHA-1 hash of predictable and low-entropy sources,…

item.title: CVE-2026-11373 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Net::Statsite::Client versions through 1.1.0 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsite::Client versions through 1.1.0 for Perl allow metric injections. Net::Statsite::Client is a client for the statsite protocol, which is a variant of statsd. Newlines are not removed from metric names, allowing metric injections. Values a…

item.title: CVE-2026-9265 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions before 1.96 for Perl permits a heap OOB read in print_attribute UTF8STRING path
item.description: Crypt::OpenSSL::PKCS12 versions before 1.96 for Perl permits a heap OOB read in print_attribute UTF8STRING path. print_attribute() copies a UTF8STRING ASN.1 attribute value into a heap buffer sized exactly to its declared length via strncpy, leaving…

item.title: CVE-2026-9692 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Mojolicious::Sessions::Storable versions through 0.05 for Perl generate session ids insecurely
item.description: Mojolicious::Sessions::Storable versions through 0.05 for Perl generate session ids insecurely. The default session id generator returns a SHA-1 hash seeded with the built-in rand function, the epoch time, the heap address of an anonymous hash, and t…

item.title: CVE-2026-12205 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Crypt::DSA versions before 1.21 for Perl reused the nonce across signatures, leading to private-key recovery
item.description: Crypt::DSA versions before 1.21 for Perl reused the nonce across signatures, leading to private-key recovery. Crypt::DSA::sign caches the per-signature nonce material in the Key object without ever clearing it. The first sign() on a Key object picks…

item.title: CVE-2026-11832 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Dancer2::Plugin::Auth::OAuth versions before 0.22 for Perl default to a predictable nonce
item.description: Dancer2::Plugin::Auth::OAuth versions before 0.22 for Perl default to a predictable nonce. The default nonce was generated using an MD5 hash of the epoch time, which is predictable.

item.title: CVE-2026-12087 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Socket versions before 2.041 for Perl have an out-of-bounds heap read
item.description: Socket versions before 2.041 for Perl have an out-of-bounds heap read. In Socket.xs, pack_ip_mreq_source() checks the length of its source argument before the argument is read, so the check tests the byte length carried over from the preceding multia…

item.title: CVE-2026-11527 · 8.6 HIGH — Config::IniFiles versions before 3.001000 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of the -file argument in _make_filehandle
item.description: Config::IniFiles versions before 3.001000 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of the -file argument in _make_filehandle. Config::IniFiles::_make_filehandle opens a filename argument with Perl's 2-arg open(), so a…

item.title: CVE-2026-11526 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — GD versions before 2.86 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of filename arguments in _make_filehandle
item.description: GD versions before 2.86 for Perl allow OS command injection and file overwrite via a 2-arg open() of filename arguments in _make_filehandle. GD::Image::_make_filehandle opens a filename argument with Perl's 2-arg open(), so a filename that begins or…

item.title: CVE-2026-9641 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl have a weak default algorithm and number of iterations
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl have a weak default algorithm and number of iterations. The default algorithm is HMAC-SHA1, which should only be used for legacy systems. These versions default to using 1000 iterations. Depending on th…

item.title: CVE-2026-9638 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl generate insecure random values for salts. These versions use the built-in rand function, which is predictable and unsuitable for cryptography.

item.title: CVE-2017-20240 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl are vulnerable to timing attacks
item.description: Crypt::PBKDF2 versions before 0.261630 for Perl are vulnerable to timing attacks. These versions use Perl's built-in eq comparison. Discrepancies in timing could be used to guess the underlying derived-key.

item.title: CVE-2026-50639 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::SignalFx versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::SignalFx versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions such as dogstatsd) allow mutiple metrics, separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. Metrics::Any::Adapte…

item.title: CVE-2026-50638 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::DogStatsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::DogStatsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions such as dogstatsd) allow mutiple metrics, separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. Metrics::Any::Adapt…

item.title: CVE-2026-50637 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Metrics::Any::Adapter::Statsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections
item.description: Metrics::Any::Adapter::Statsd versions before 0.04 for Perl does not protect against metric injections. The statsd protocol (and extensions) allow mutiple metrics, separated by newlines, to be sent per packet. The send method does not validate the co…

item.title: CVE-2009-10007 — Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions before 0.10_027 for Perl is susceptible to session fixation attacks
item.description: Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication versions before 0.10_027 for Perl is susceptible to session fixation attacks. Catalyst::Plugin::Authentication does not automatically change the session id after authentication. An attacker that obtains a session id c…

item.title: CVE-2026-9698 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl saved errors in a limited-sized buffer
item.description: DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl saved errors in a limited-sized buffer. Error messages that were returned when RaiseError, PrintError or HandleError were set were written to a 200-byte buffer without a length limit. Attackers that can influence th…

item.title: CVE-2026-10725 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Protocol::HTTP2 versions before 1.13 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb
item.description: Protocol::HTTP2 versions before 1.13 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb. Protocol::HTTP2's inbound HPACK path has no header-list size limit, so a small HTTP/2 request can expand into large server memory (the "HTTP/2 bomb"). The headers_decode me…

item.title: CVE-2026-11362 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections from event tags
item.description: DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections from event tags. DataDog::DogStatsd does not properly sanitise input, allowing metric injections of data from untrusted sources. The format_event method (used by the event meth…

item.title: CVE-2026-9270 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: DataDog::DogStatsd versions through 0.07 for Perl allow metric injections. DataDog::DogStatsd does not properly sanitise input, allowing metric injections of data from untrusted sources. The send_stats method does not remove newlines from metric name…

item.title: CVE-2026-10879 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl have a heap overflow when preparsing SQL statements with more than 9 binders
item.description: DBI versions before 1.648 for Perl have a heap overflow when preparsing SQL statements with more than 9 binders. The preparse method expands SQL placeholder characters to numbered binders of the form :pN, but only allocates three characters per binde…

item.title: CVE-2026-49942 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate network masks
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate network masks. The mask portion of a network mask could contain Unicode digits such as the Arabic-Indic One (U+0661), or non-digits, which were ignored. This could allow network masks to…

item.title: CVE-2026-49941 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate IP addresses
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl did not validate IP addresses. The add method called the _encode method to parse addresses. If the addresses did not look like netmasks or network ranges, then they were assumed to single IP addresses and…

item.title: CVE-2026-49940 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl accept non-ASCII IP addresses and netmasks
item.description: Net::CIDR::Set versions through 0.20 for Perl accept non-ASCII IP addresses and netmasks. Unicode digits such as the Arabic-Indic One (U+0661) were accepted but not properly parsed as numbers. This could allow network masks to accept larger networks.

item.title: CVE-2026-46741 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Etsy::StatsD versions through 1.002002 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Etsy::StatsD versions through 1.002002 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names and values are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. Note that the git rep…

item.title: CVE-2026-46739 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::Statsd versions before 0.13 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Net::Statsd versions before 0.13 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics. The update_stats (used for updating co…

item.title: CVE-2026-8829 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — HTML::Entities versions before 3.84 for Perl read freed heap memory in _decode_entities
item.description: HTML::Entities versions before 3.84 for Perl read freed heap memory in _decode_entities. The XS routine backing HTML::Entities::_decode_entities cached a pointer (repl) into the entity-value SV returned by hv_fetch on the entity2char hash. When the i…

item.title: CVE-2026-8722 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Net::Async::Statsd::Client versions through 0.005 for Perl allow metric injections
item.description: Net::Async::Statsd::Client versions through 0.005 for Perl allow metric injections. The metric names are not checked for newlines, colons or pipes. Metrics generated from untrusted sources could inject additional statsd metrics.

item.title: CVE-2026-9516 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow denial of service via UTF-8 BOM prefixed input when a decode filter callback throws
item.description: Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow denial of service via UTF-8 BOM prefixed input when a decode filter callback throws. To skip a leading 3-byte UTF-8 BOM, decode_json() advances the input scalar's string pointer past the mark with…

item.title: CVE-2026-9334 · 7.3 HIGH 🌩️ — Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow type confusion via duplicate object keys when dupkeys_as_arrayref is enabled
item.description: Cpanel::JSON::XS versions before 4.41 for Perl allow type confusion via duplicate object keys when dupkeys_as_arrayref is enabled. decode_hv() collapses duplicate object keys into an array reference under dupkeys_as_arrayref. The branch reached for a…

item.title: CVE-2026-8796 · 8.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input
item.description: Sereal::Decoder versions before 5.005 for Perl allow heap out-of-bounds read via crafted input. In Perl/Decoder/srl_decoder.c, srl_read_object() and srl_read_hash() process a COPY tag, a back-reference whose target byte the decoder re-decodes as a fr…

item.title: CVE-2026-8594 · 6.2 MEDIUM — Text::LineFold versions through 2019.001 for Perl duplicate the output based on the number of special break characters
item.description: Text::LineFold versions through 2019.001 for Perl duplicate the output based on the number of special break characters. Text::LineFold splits the input string by specific line break characters (such as VT, FF and others) into segments, but applies th…

item.title: CVE-2026-41565 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl have a stack buffer overflow in four AEAD decrypt_verify helpers
item.description: CryptX versions before 0.088_001 for Perl have a stack buffer overflow in four AEAD decrypt_verify helpers. The gcm_decrypt_verify, ccm_decrypt_verify, chacha20poly1305_decrypt_verify and eax_decrypt_verify XS routines copied the caller-supplied auth…
